System

C8 Health Design System

Welcome to Panda 3.0

The C8 Health design system turns clinical guidance into experiences built for clarity and meaningful action. It keeps what matters legible and accessible, so the right information stands out, is understood quickly, and is acted on with confidence.

Last updated June 16, 2026

How the system gets its color: a small set of raw primitive ramps, wrapped by a rich layer of semantic roles that carry meaning and adapt across Light and Dark. Components bind the semantic roles, never the raw ramps.

3-layerprimitive rampssemantic rolescomponent bindings

Overview - what this group is

Color is the only category with a full three-layer chain. The bottom layer is the raw ramps - gray, blue, the status hues, plus the neutral overlay ramp and the two base values (white and black). These are pure pigment with no meaning attached. On top sits the semantic layer (color.text.*, color.surface.*, color.border.*, color.action.*, color.feedback.*): named roles that say what a color is for, and that carry a different value in Light and Dark. A component asks for color.text.secondary and gets the right gray for the current mode without knowing which ramp step that is.

The mode adaptation lives entirely in the semantic layer. A primitive like gray.600 is one fixed value; the role text.secondary points at gray.600 in Light and gray.400 in Dark. That is the whole reason the semantic layer exists.

Relationships & usage

How the hierarchy flows for color, and what binds each token.

Read a row left to right: a raw value, the named role(s) that give it meaning and Light/Dark adaptation, then the components that bind it. A dashed tag means there is no semantic alias - components bind the primitive directly.

When & how to use

Pick the role that names your intent, then let the mode resolve the value.

Do

  • Bind components to a semantic role (color.action.primary.background), so Dark just works.
  • Reach for a status role (color.feedback.error.*) when the color carries meaning, not a raw hue.
  • Mint a new semantic role when no existing role fits your intent (border.control, D-111).

Don't

  • Bind a raw ramp step (color.blue.600) from a component - that skips the mode layer and breaks Dark.
  • Invent a literal hex anywhere in a component - every color resolves from a token.
  • Use a text color role as a control border; non-text contrast (3:1) is a different bar (why border.control was minted).

Accessibility notes

  • Text roles target WCAG 2.2 AA (4.5:1, or 3:1 for large text) on every surface they pair with, Light and Dark.
  • Non-text roles that are the sole cue of a control (border.control) clear the 3:1 bar of WCAG 1.4.11 - a control border cannot reuse a text role.
  • Color is never the only state cue (D-055): the check glyph, dot, or label carries selection; the fill only reinforces it (WCAG 1.4.1).
  • Status fills (destructive) carry a white label verified AA across rest/hover/active (5.22 / 6.06 / 7.06:1).

Token reference

Every token in this group with its resolved value, alias chain, and live "used by" links. The Lens toggle leads with semantic roles (use) or with primitives (architecture).

Type is special: primitives are the raw axes (family, weight, size, line-height, letter-spacing), and the semantic layer is multi-axis recipes (ui.body, heading.1) that bundle them into a ready-to-apply style.

3-layerprimitive axessemantic recipescomponent bindings

Font Awesome 6 Sharp (icons-as-text, D-044) - inline-SVG preview, kit not loaded

Trash
Copy
Edit
Tag
Search

Overview - what this group is

Typography has the same primitive / semantic / component shape as color, but the semantic layer is a recipe rather than a single value: heading.1 bundles family Inter, weight bold, size 30, line-height snug, and letter-spacing tight. The primitive axes are independent ladders (font.size 12-72, font.weight 300-700, line-height, letter-spacing). Components and the viewer bind a recipe (ui.button-md, ui.input-sm), never a loose axis. Two families only: Inter for UI, EB Garamond 400 upright for display.

Because a recipe is multi-axis, the value of a type token is a bundle - which is why the facts panel shows family / weight / size / line-height per recipe. Edit the sample text, not the recipe: the style stays token-bound.

Relationships & usage

How the hierarchy flows for typography, and what binds each token.

Read a row left to right: a raw value, the named role(s) that give it meaning and Light/Dark adaptation, then the components that bind it. A dashed tag means there is no semantic alias - components bind the primitive directly.

font.letter-spacing.tight-0.02em
font.letter-spacing.normal0em
font.letter-spacing.wide0.015em
font.letter-spacing.caps0.18em

When & how to use

Bind a named recipe; never assemble loose axes in a component.

Do

  • Bind a recipe (typography.ui.body, typography.heading.1) for any text.
  • Use Inter for UI and EB Garamond regular 400 upright for display only.
  • Mint a recipe when a new role recurs (ui.caption, ui.field-label).

Don't

  • Assemble font-size, weight, and line-height by hand in a component - that is what a recipe is for.
  • Use EB Garamond heavier than 400 or italic.
  • Reconstruct an uppercase eyebrow from primitives (use ui.caption or the overline recipe).

Accessibility notes

  • Body recipes carry adequate line-height (ui.body 150%) for readability (supports WCAG 1.4.8 / 1.4.12 text spacing).
  • Type roles are themeless; the pairing color role is what must clear contrast in each mode.
  • Recipes keep size and weight relationships consistent so hierarchy is perceivable, not color-dependent.

Token reference

Every token in this group with its resolved value, alias chain, and live "used by" links. The Lens toggle leads with semantic roles (use) or with primitives (architecture).

Semantic roles

Edit any sample text inline; press Enter for a line break. The recipe stays token-bound; edits reset on reload.

The padding, gap, and rhythm scale. A 4px-based ladder of primitive steps, with a thin semantic layout layer (layout.stack-*, layout.inset-*) naming the common rhythms. Most components bind a spacing step directly.

2-3 layerprimitive ladderthin layout layerdirect binding

Overview - what this group is

Spacing is a single primitive ladder built on a 4px grid: spacing.1 = 4px up to spacing.20 = 80px, with the mid-scale steps (2, 4, 6) doing most of the work. Unlike color there is no per-mode adaptation - a gap is the same in Light and Dark - so the semantic layer here is thinner: layout.json names the recurring rhythms (stack-sm, inset-card, grid-gutter) by aliasing a spacing step. Components may bind a layout role for page rhythm, or bind a spacing step directly for a local gap (Space gaps to spacing.2/4/6, D-108).

The number is the grid index, not the pixel count: spacing.4 is the 4th meaningful step (16px), not 4px. The ladder deliberately skips values (no 7, 9, 11) so the scale stays coarse and consistent; a literal px gap is forbidden - if a step is missing, mint one rather than hardcode.

Relationships & usage

How the hierarchy flows for spacing, and what binds each token.

Primitive (raw value)Semantic role(s) that alias itBound by
spacing.28pxlayout.gap-inlinelayout.stack-smAutocompleteButtonCheckboxDividerInputInputnumberRadioSelectSpaceSwitch
spacing.312pxbound directly (no semantic alias)AutocompleteButtonInputInputnumberSelect
spacing.416pxlayout.inset-cardlayout.inset-card-compactlayout.stack-mdAutocompleteButtonDividerInputInputnumberSelectSpace
spacing.624pxlayout.grid-gutterlayout.inset-cardlayout.page-padding-blocklayout.page-padding-inlinelayout.section-gaplayout.stack-lgButtonDividerSpace

Read a row left to right: a raw value, the named role(s) that give it meaning and Light/Dark adaptation, then the components that bind it. A dashed tag means there is no semantic alias - components bind the primitive directly.

Live preview

Pick a spacing token for the card padding and for the gap between its blocks, and watch it apply live - the card binds the chosen token (nothing hardcoded) and themes with the mode toggle.

Padding
Gap
Protocol summary
A synthetic preview card. Pick a spacing token above and watch the card padding and the gap between these blocks update live.

padding spacing.4 (16px) · gap spacing.3 (12px)

When & how to use

Choose by rhythm role: hairline pairs, default stack, section break.

Do

  • Bind a spacing step (spacing.4) or a layout role (layout.stack-md) - never a literal px.
  • Use the thin layout layer for page-level rhythm so the intent is named (inset-card, grid-gutter).
  • Flag a mint when the layout genuinely needs a step the ladder skips.

Don't

  • Hardcode 15px because it looks right - snap to the nearest ladder step or mint.
  • Treat the token number as pixels (spacing.6 is 24px, not 6px).
  • Add 96 or 128 ad hoc; the ceiling is 80px until a layout proves the need.

Accessibility notes

  • Spacing is presentational; visual order must follow DOM order so it is meaningful to assistive tech (WCAG 1.3.2).
  • Adequate gap around interactive controls supports the 24px / 44px target rules (WCAG 2.5.8 / 2.5.5) handled in the sizing scale.

Token reference

Every token in this group with its resolved value, alias chain, and live "used by" links. The Lens toggle leads with semantic roles (use) or with primitives (architecture).

Control heights, icon boxes, avatar sizes, and the content container width. These are the fixed dimensions that make controls land on a consistent rhythm and meet touch-target rules.

2 layerprimitive stepsdirect binding

Overview - what this group is

Sizing groups four sub-ladders: size.control (xs 24 / sm 28 / md 36 / lg 44 - the height a Button or field renders at), size.icon (the glyph box, 16-32), size.avatar, and size.container.content (the 1152px centred max width, aliased by layout.content-max). Control and icon sizes are paired by component specs so the glyph sits one optical step inside its control. There is little mode adaptation here, so components bind the primitive step directly via their spec size map.

Control height is the load-bearing accessibility dimension: md (36px) clears the 24px minimum target (WCAG 2.5.8) and lg (44px) is the only step meeting the enhanced 44px touch target (2.5.5). The icon ladder tracks one step below the box so glyphs never crowd their frame.

Relationships & usage

How the hierarchy flows for sizing, and what binds each token.

Primitive (raw value)Semantic role(s) that alias itBound by
size.icon.xs16pxbound directly (no semantic alias)CheckboxRadio
size.icon.sm20pxbound directly (no semantic alias)CheckboxRadio
size.icon.md24pxbound directly (no semantic alias)CheckboxRadio
size.control.xs24pxbound directly (no semantic alias)Button
size.control.sm28pxbound directly (no semantic alias)AutocompleteButtonInputInputnumberSelect
size.control.md36pxbound directly (no semantic alias)AutocompleteButtonInputInputnumberSelect
size.control.lg44pxbound directly (no semantic alias)AutocompleteButtonInputInputnumberSelect
size.switch.default.track-width44pxbound directly (no semantic alias)Switch
size.switch.default.track-height24pxbound directly (no semantic alias)Switch
size.switch.default.thumb20pxbound directly (no semantic alias)Switch
size.switch.small.track-width28pxbound directly (no semantic alias)Switch
size.switch.small.track-height16pxbound directly (no semantic alias)Switch
size.switch.small.thumb12pxbound directly (no semantic alias)Switch
size.container.content1152pxlayout.content-maxnot yet bound

size.avatar.md32px

size.avatar.lg40px
Unreferencedprimitive nothing references (a real smell)
size.container.content1152px

When & how to use

Pick a control height for the density; the icon box follows from the spec.

Do

  • Use size.control.md as the default action height; reserve lg for touch and mobile bars.
  • Pair the icon box from the component spec (xs box to 12px glyph) rather than guessing.
  • Bind size.container.content via layout.content-max for centred page width.

Don't

  • Use xs (24px) as a touch target - it is desktop-dense only.
  • Hardcode a control height; bind the size step.
  • Mix icon box and glyph sizes outside the paired ladder.

Accessibility notes

  • size.control.md (36) clears WCAG 2.5.8 (24px minimum target).
  • size.control.lg (44) is the only step meeting WCAG 2.5.5 (44px enhanced target) - use it for primary touch surfaces.
  • xs (24) is desktop-dense; never the sole touch affordance.

Token reference

Corner rounding steps, from a deliberate square reset to the pill sentinel. Small set, used everywhere a surface has a corner.

2 layerprimitive stepsdirect binding

Overview - what this group is

Five steps: none (an explicit square, so 'no radius' is a documented decision not an omission), sm (6px, small / dense elements), md (8px, the default for buttons, inputs, selects), lg (12px, large surfaces like cards and modals), and full (9999px, a sentinel that produces a pill - not a measured dimension). Components bind a radius step directly; the menu inset rows (D-103) pair radius.sm inside a radius.md menu.

full is a sentinel, not a real measurement - do not treat 9999 as a dimension to compute with. none is intentional, not missing: it records a square decision.

Relationships & usage

How the hierarchy flows for radius, and what binds each token.

Primitive (raw value)Semantic role(s) that alias itBound by
radius.sm6pxbound directly (no semantic alias)ButtonCheckboxTooltip
radius.md8pxbound directly (no semantic alias)AutocompleteButtonColorpickerDatepickerInputInputnumberNotificationPopconfirmPopoverSelectTimepicker
radius.lg12pxbound directly (no semantic alias)DrawerModal
radius.full9999pxbound directly (no semantic alias)MessageRadioSwitch

Read a row left to right: a raw value, the named role(s) that give it meaning and Light/Dark adaptation, then the components that bind it. A dashed tag means there is no semantic alias - components bind the primitive directly.

The scale

radius.none0px
Unreferencedprimitive nothing references (a real smell)
radius.sm6px
Used bybutton xs / radiuscheckbox box-radiusoption-row radiustooltip panel-radius
radius.md8px
Used byautocomplete radiusbutton sm / radiusbutton md / radiusbutton lg / radiuscolorpicker panel-radiusdatepicker panel-radiusinput radiusinputnumber radiusnotification panel-radiuspopconfirm panel-radiuspopover panel-radiusselect radiustimepicker panel-radius
radius.lg12px
Used bydrawer panel-radiusmodal panel-radius
radius.full9999px
Used bymessage panel-radiusradio box-radiusswitch track-radiusswitch thumb-radius

When & how to use

Match the radius to the surface scale.

Do

  • Use radius.md for standard controls (buttons, inputs, selects).
  • Use radius.lg for large containers (cards, panels, modals).
  • Use radius.full for pills, chips, avatars, capsule toggles.

Don't

  • Compute with 9999 - it is a pill sentinel, not a measured value.
  • Hardcode a corner; bind a radius step.
  • Mix radii on nested surfaces without intent (the inset menu rows are a deliberate sm-in-md pairing).

Accessibility notes

  • Radius is decorative and carries no contrast or interaction meaning; no specific WCAG criterion applies.

Token reference

Every token in this group with its resolved value, alias chain, and live "used by" links. The Lens toggle leads with semantic roles (use) or with primitives (architecture).

Primitives

radius.none0px
Unreferencedprimitive nothing references (a real smell)
radius.sm6px
Used bybutton xs / radiuscheckbox box-radiusoption-row radiustooltip panel-radius
radius.md8px
Used byautocomplete radiusbutton sm / radiusbutton md / radiusbutton lg / radiuscolorpicker panel-radiusdatepicker panel-radiusinput radiusinputnumber radiusnotification panel-radiuspopconfirm panel-radiuspopover panel-radiusselect radiustimepicker panel-radius
radius.lg12px
Used bydrawer panel-radiusmodal panel-radius
radius.full9999px

Stroke weights. Just three steps, but they carry meaning: the hairline structures everything, the strong weight is reserved for focus and emphasis.

2 layerprimitive stepsdirect binding

Overview - what this group is

Three steps: none (an explicit borderless reset), default (1px - the structural hairline behind almost every divider, field, card, and table rule), and strong (2px - the focus ring and selected / emphasis outlines). The strong weight pairs with color.border.focus; the color lives in the semantic layer while the weight is the primitive. Components bind the width step directly.

The strong (2px) weight is an accessibility choice, not a style flourish: a thicker focus ring is more perceivable for low-vision users. It is reserved for focus and emphasis so a 2px edge always reads as 'this is focused or selected'.

Relationships & usage

How the hierarchy flows for border width, and what binds each token.

Primitive (raw value)Semantic role(s) that alias itBound by
border.width.default1pxbound directly (no semantic alias)AutocompleteButtonDividerInputInputnumberSelect
border.width.strong2pxbound directly (no semantic alias)AutocompleteButtonCheckboxInputInputnumberRadioSelectSwitch

Read a row left to right: a raw value, the named role(s) that give it meaning and Light/Dark adaptation, then the components that bind it. A dashed tag means there is no semantic alias - components bind the primitive directly.

The scale

border.width.none0px
Unreferencedprimitive nothing references (a real smell)
border.width.default1px
Used byautocomplete borderWidthbutton xs / border-widthbutton sm / border-widthbutton md / border-widthbutton lg / border-widthdivider line-widthinput borderWidthinputnumber stepper-divider-widthinputnumber borderWidthselect borderWidth
border.width.strong2px
Used byautocomplete widthbutton widthcheckbox box-edgecheckbox widthinput widthinputnumber widthradio box-edgeradio widthselect widthswitch width

When & how to use

Hairline for structure, strong for focus and emphasis only.

Do

  • Use border.width.default for all structural edges (dividers, fields, cards).
  • Reserve border.width.strong for the focus ring and selected outlines.
  • Pair strong with color.border.focus for the ring.

Don't

  • Use 2px for ordinary structure - it then stops signalling focus.
  • Hardcode a stroke; bind the width step.
  • Omit a border where 'none' is the intent without using border.width.none.

Accessibility notes

  • border.width.strong (2px) makes the focus indicator more perceivable for low-vision users (supports WCAG 2.4.7 / 2.4.13 focus appearance).
  • Reserving the strong weight for focus keeps the focus state unambiguous.

Token reference

Every token in this group with its resolved value, alias chain, and live "used by" links. The Lens toggle leads with semantic roles (use) or with primitives (architecture).

Semantic roles

focus.ring.offset2px
focus.ring.offset => 2px
Used byautocomplete offsetbutton offsetcheckbox offsetinput offsetinputnumber offsetradio offsetselect offsetswitch offset
focus.ring.spread2px
focus.ring.spread => 2px
Used byautocomplete spreadbutton spreadcheckbox spreadinput spreadinputnumber spreadradio spreadselect spreadswitch spread

Primitives

border.width.none0px
Unreferencedprimitive nothing references (a real smell)
border.width.default1px
Used byautocomplete borderWidthbutton xs / border-widthbutton sm / border-widthbutton md / border-widthbutton lg / border-widthdivider line-widthinput borderWidthinputnumber stepper-divider-widthinputnumber borderWidthselect borderWidth
border.width.strong2px
Used byautocomplete widthbutton widthcheckbox box-edgecheckbox widthinput widthinputnumber widthradio box-edgeradio widthselect widthswitch width

Transition durations. Four steps from instant (the reduced-motion target) to slow, matched to how far a surface travels.

2-3 layerprimitive durationssemantic rolesdirect binding

Overview - what this group is

Motion is a small ladder of durations in milliseconds: instant (0 - snap states and the reduced-motion target), fast (150 - hover / focus / press), base (200 - popovers, dropdowns, toasts, modal fade), and slow (300 - drawers and side panels that travel further). A thin semantic motion layer names roles bound by components; the principle is that bigger surfaces moving more distance get a longer duration.

instant (0ms) is not 'no animation by accident' - it is the explicit target every transform-based motion collapses to under prefers-reduced-motion. Duration scales with travel distance, not importance.

Relationships & usage

How the hierarchy flows for motion, and what binds each token.

Primitive (raw value)Semantic role(s) that alias itBound by
motion.duration.fast150msmotion.hovermotion.modal-exitmotion.pressnot yet bound
motion.duration.base200msmotion.modal-entermotion.popovermotion.toastnot yet bound
motion.duration.slow300msmotion.drawermotion.skeletonnot yet bound

Read a row left to right: a raw value, the named role(s) that give it meaning and Light/Dark adaptation, then the components that bind it. A dashed tag means there is no semantic alias - components bind the primitive directly.

The scale

motion.duration.instant0ms
Unreferencedprimitive nothing references (a real smell)
motion.duration.fast150ms
motion.duration.base200ms
motion.duration.slow300ms

When & how to use

Match duration to how far the thing moves.

Do

  • Use fast (150) for micro-interactions: hover, focus, press.
  • Use base (200) for popovers, dropdowns, toasts, modal fade.
  • Collapse transform motion to instant (0) under prefers-reduced-motion.

Don't

  • Use slow (300) for a hover - it feels laggy.
  • Hardcode a duration in ms; bind a motion role.
  • Animate with motion as the only cue of a state change.

Accessibility notes

  • instant (0ms) is the reduced-motion target: all transform-based motion must collapse to it under prefers-reduced-motion (WCAG 2.3.3).
  • Motion is never the sole indicator of a state change (WCAG 1.4.1 reasoning extended to motion).

Token reference

Every token in this group with its resolved value, alias chain, and live "used by" links. The Lens toggle leads with semantic roles (use) or with primitives (architecture).

Semantic roles

motion.hover150ms
motion.hover -> motion.duration.fast => 150ms
Not yet consumedno component binds this yet (expected this early)
motion.press150ms
motion.press -> motion.duration.fast => 150ms
Not yet consumedno component binds this yet (expected this early)
motion.popover200ms
motion.popover -> motion.duration.base => 200ms
Not yet consumedno component binds this yet (expected this early)
motion.toast200ms
motion.toast -> motion.duration.base => 200ms
Not yet consumedno component binds this yet (expected this early)
motion.modal-enter200ms
motion.modal-enter -> motion.duration.base => 200ms
Not yet consumedno component binds this yet (expected this early)
motion.modal-exit150ms
motion.modal-exit -> motion.duration.fast => 150ms
Not yet consumedno component binds this yet (expected this early)
motion.drawer300ms
motion.drawer -> motion.duration.slow => 300ms
Not yet consumedno component binds this yet (expected this early)
motion.skeleton300ms
motion.skeleton -> motion.duration.slow => 300ms
Not yet consumedno component binds this yet (expected this early)

Primitives

motion.duration.instant0ms
Unreferencedprimitive nothing references (a real smell)
motion.duration.fast150ms
motion.duration.base200ms
motion.duration.slow300ms

Every icon the system ships, rendered from the canonical source (icons/ + icons.manifest.json, D-145) and read at build time from build/icons.resolved.json, so this page cannot drift from what components render.

Source icons/Generated build/icons/*Consumed by components

Overview - the icon system

Font Awesome 7 Sharp Pro (regular 400) curated subset + C8 brand. Icons render as real inline single-path SVG (D-142): the path uses fill="currentColor" so each glyph inherits the text or label color of the slot it sits in (D-143; the default UI affordance color is color.text.secondary per D-102), and the rendered size comes from the typography/icon/* recipe applied as width and height (D-144). The set is a single weight: Font Awesome Sharp regular 400 is the universal default glyph weight (D-072/D-146).

Status reflects the manifest: active means part of the curated set in use; available means seeded for a planned need.

UI icons (23)

Single-path FA glyphs, currentColor-ready, viewBox normalized to 0 0 W 512. Shown here in color.text.primary on the page surface; in product they inherit the color of the consuming slot.

Brand (1)

The C8 symbol is brand artwork (D-148): it keeps its intrinsic color (never recolored to the theme), is not FA-code-named, and is not recipe-sized, so it is exempt from the single-path / currentColor / viewBox-512 rules the FA glyphs follow.

  • C8 Healthactive
    c8-symbol
    Category
    brand
    Custom
    yes
    viewBox
    0 0 1153 1153
    Since
    0.36.0

Accessibility

  • Decorative versus meaningful is set at the consuming slot, not baked into the asset (D-147): decorative icons take aria-hidden="true"; meaningful icons take role="img" plus a <title> / aria-label drawn from the manifest title.
  • Color is never the sole state cue (D-055): the option-row check, the box-glyph check, and the switch thumb position stay load-bearing, reinforced by shape and text.
  • Icons are non-text graphics, so the icon-to-surface relationship is not a WCAG text-contrast requirement; the brand mark is exempt from contrast (WCAG 1.4.3 / 1.4.11).

Feedback / composite component (banner, D-159..D-162): the showcase shows every authored form. Status is conveyed by icon shape + text, never color alone (WCAG 1.4.1); no interaction-state gallery.

Floating surface (overlay model, D-153): placement anchored, scrim off, elevation overlay, focus none. Open it from the trigger to preview; no interaction-state gallery.

plainboolean (default on). One label recipe (ui/caption); plain renders the label as a quiet caption rather than an emphasized heading. labelPosition is only meaningful on a horizontal rule with a label.

Layout primitive: structural axes only, no interaction states (D-107). Every authored form is shown in the showcase.

Bindings (from the component spec)

Inter 400 / 14px / 150%

Box-glyph (D-113): the check / minus glyph is the load-bearing state cue, never fill color alone (D-055, WCAG 1.4.1); the resting box border clears WCAG 1.4.11 (3:1, color.border.control).

UX guidance

When to use

Use an alert for an inline, contextual status message tied to a region or action, such as a save result, an expiring-items notice, or a validation summary, placed where the status applies.

When not to use

Not for a transient confirmation, which is a Message or toast; not for a blocking decision, which is a Modal; not for a full-page outcome, which is a Result.

Behavior

The status is carried by the surface tint, the border, the icon shape, and the text together, never by color alone (WCAG 1.4.1). An error is assertive (role=alert) while success, warning, and info are polite (role=status). A dismissible alert keeps a named close. In Dark the tints collapse to the gray.800 feedback surface with gray.50 text (D-059).

Keyboard

If the alert is dismissible, Tab reaches the close button, which carries an accessible name; the alert surface itself is not focusable.

Content

Keep the message to one clear sentence, lead with what happened, and offer the next action as a button or link. Use synthetic content in any example.

Do

  • ✓Pair every status with an icon and text
  • ✓Use role=alert only for errors
  • ✓Keep the message to a sentence
  • ✓Give the close an accessible name

Don't

  • ✕Rely on color alone for status
  • ✕Use an Alert for a transient toast
  • ✕Stack many alerts
  • ✕Use an Alert for a blocking decision

When to use

Use AutoComplete for a free-text value where suggestions help but the user may type something not listed; keep the field plain (a search box is the Input search variant, D-080). Good for a text field where past or known entries speed entry without constraining the value.

When not to use

Not when the value must come from a closed set, which is a Select; not for picking several, which is multi-select; not for a pure search with no free-text result (use the Input search variant). If the suggestions are a hard constraint on the value, it is a Select, not AutoComplete. At the sm size the value text is 12px (D-170): reserve sm fields for dense, low-stakes desktop UI and do NOT use them for high-stakes data entry such as clinically important values; on mobile, sub-16px field text triggers iOS Safari auto-zoom (D-068), so re-floor field text at 16px there.

Behavior

Typing filters the suggestions and the typed substring is emphasized so matches are easy to scan (Baymard); selecting a suggestion fills the field and closes the menu, free text commits as typed, and a readable 'No matches' line confirms an empty result rather than a blank panel. The emphasis is a visual aid, never the only cue (WCAG 1.3.1). The menu reuses the Select surface and rows at rest and hover only - there is no persistent selection, so no check gutter is shown. Reduced motion drops the menu open animation (D-037).

Keyboard

ArrowDown and ArrowUp move the active suggestion, Enter accepts the active one, Escape closes the menu and keeps the typed text; typing continues to filter. The control exposes the editable combobox role and states (role=combobox, aria-expanded, aria-controls, aria-autocomplete=list), the menu is the listbox, and suggestions are options (WAI-ARIA APG editable combobox).

Content

Use a real example placeholder, never the label (NN/g); keep suggestions short and scannable; keep 'No matches' as the canonical empty string. The placeholder is a genuine example of a value, not instructions.

Do

  • ✓Keep the field plain and allow free text
  • ✓Emphasize the typed match in each suggestion
  • ✓Show a readable No matches line
  • ✓Reuse the Select menu rows at rest and hover

Don't

  • ✕Constrain the value to the suggestions - that is Select
  • ✕Show the check gutter (there is no persistent selection)
  • ✕Use text.disabled for the empty row (it fails AA in Dark)
  • ✕Bake a search icon into AutoComplete - use the Input search variant

When to use

Trigger an action or submit - not navigation (that is the Link text role). Primary is the single highest-emphasis action on a screen; use exactly one. Secondary carries supporting actions (it absorbed the old Outline, D-041). Ghost is low-emphasis for toolbars, tables, and icon affordances - transparent until hover. Destructive is an intent overlay for irreversible or dangerous actions, not a rung on the emphasis ladder. Default size is md (36), which clears the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target (24px); use lg (44) for the enhanced 2.5.5 target (44px) in touch and mobile/clinical-tablet contexts (D-068). For destructive actions prefer an undo affordance over a confirmation prompt where the action is reversible; reserve a confirmation for irreversible or high-cost actions, keep the destructive control spatially separated from the benign control beside it, and gate the most dangerous behind a deliberate confirm such as typing a word (NN/g).

When not to use

Not for inline navigation styled as text (use Link, no button geometry). Not for choosing from a set or toggling a setting (Select, Checkbox, Radio, Switch). Avoid more than one Primary per view - competing primaries flatten the emphasis ladder.

Behavior

Rest is authored; hover, active, and disabled are code-applied (D-048); focus is the shared 2px ring overlay (D-040) outside the box so focusing never shifts layout. Disabled suppresses the focus ring and is exempt from AA contrast (D-014). Color transitions animate at motion/hover; any press transform drops to instant under reduced motion, and state is never conveyed by motion alone. Icon-only is a label-less square at any size - the regular button at that size with the glyph centered and no text (D-176); it needs an accessible name. Prefer not to disable an action button; where the action is unavailable, allow the click and surface an error that says what to fix, rather than a silently disabled control that gives no reason (NN/g). When a button must be disabled, convey why in adjacent text. Loading keeps an accessible name and sets aria-busy, blocks re-activation while in flight, and is announced through a live region (role=status), never by spinner motion alone (D-037, D-093).

Keyboard

A native button: Tab moves focus (the ring appears), Enter or Space activates; disabled leaves the tab order. Activation fires the action, it does not navigate.

Content

Labels are short, sentence-case verb phrases that name the action (Save, Add patient, Delete). Leading and trailing glyphs are opt-in (both off by default), size-track the label (D-076), and inherit its color via currentColor (D-044). An icon-only button needs an accessible name.

Do

  • ✓Use exactly one Primary as the main action on a screen
  • ✓Reach for lg (44) where the enhanced 2.5.5 touch target (44px) applies
  • ✓Give an icon-only button an accessible name
  • ✓Let icon color inherit the label (currentColor)
  • ✓Use Ghost for low-emphasis toolbar and in-table actions
  • ✓Offer undo for a reversible destructive action instead of a confirmation prompt
  • ✓Keep an accessible name on a loading button and announce the state, do not rely on the spinner

Don't

  • ✕Use a button for navigation - that is the Link text role
  • ✕Place competing Primary buttons in the same view
  • ✕Convey state through motion alone
  • ✕Hardcode a corner radius - all sizes bind radius/md
  • ✕Reach past the semantic layer to a primitive
  • ✕Disable an action button with no visible reason - allow the click and explain the error instead
  • ✕Place a destructive action flush beside its benign neighbor (NN/g proximity)

When to use

Use a checkbox for an independent on/off choice or to pick several non-exclusive options from a set, and a single checkbox for one binary agreement that is submitted with a form; use the indeterminate state only for a parent that controls a mixed group (WAI-ARIA APG checkbox / tri-state).

When not to use

Not for mutually exclusive options, which belong in a Radio group (NN/g); not for a setting that takes effect immediately with no submit, which is a Switch (NN/g toggle guidance); not for choosing from a long enumerable list, which is a Select.

Behavior

The checked state is carried by the check glyph, never by the fill color alone (D-055, WCAG 1.4.1), so it survives greyscale and color-blindness; hover, focus, and disabled are code-applied and focus is the shared 2px ring (D-040); the unchecked box border is deliberately darker than a quiet divider because the box boundary is the only cue that a control is there (WCAG 1.4.11, color.border.control). Indeterminate is a visual parent state, not a third value the user sets (APG). Prefer not to disable a checkbox; where it must be disabled, convey why (D-097).

Keyboard

Each checkbox is a tab stop and Space toggles it; the label is part of the click target; expose the native checkbox role with aria-checked true, false, or mixed for indeterminate (APG).

Content

Label in sentence case, phrased positively for what checking does (Send shift reminders, not a negative), one idea per box, with the label itself clickable (NN/g); a required single checkbox states the requirement in the field-group helper, not in the box.

Do

  • ✓Use a checkbox for independent on/off and multi-select choices
  • ✓Phrase the label positively and in sentence case
  • ✓Keep the glyph as the checked cue, not color alone
  • ✓Reserve indeterminate for a parent of a mixed group
  • ✓Make the whole row a click target that meets the touch minimum

Don't

  • ✕Use a checkbox for mutually exclusive choices - that is Radio
  • ✕Use a checkbox for a setting that applies immediately - that is Switch
  • ✕Rely on fill color alone to show checked
  • ✕Phrase the label as a negative
  • ✕Disable a checkbox with no reason shown (D-097)

When to use

Use a color picker to choose a color value such as a category or tag color or a theme accent, picking from the 2D area and hue or typing an exact hex, with presets offered for the common choices.

When not to use

Not for choosing from a small fixed set of named options, which is a Select or a Radio group; not for anything that is not a color.

Behavior

The swatch trigger opens an anchored color popover that closes on Escape or outside-click and flips to stay on-screen, with no scrim and no trap (the overlay model, D-155). A hex input is always available as the precise, keyboard-complete path, so the 2D area is an enhancement and never the only way in. Markers are double-ringed - a white ring with a border.control outline - so they stay visible on any underlying color (WCAG 1.4.11), and the selected preset is shown by a ring plus its position, never color alone (WCAG 1.4.1). Motion follows D-037.

Keyboard

Open the trigger; in the panel the hex input accepts a typed value, the hue is an ARIA slider operated with the arrow keys and announced via aria-valuetext, and the area is arrow-operable; Escape closes and returns focus to the trigger.

Content

Show the hex value by the swatch, give the trigger an accessible name such as Tag color, and use synthetic values in any example.

Do

  • ✓Always offer the hex input as the keyboard path
  • ✓Double-ring the markers so they show on any color
  • ✓Put a border.control edge on every swatch
  • ✓Mark the selected preset with a ring, not color alone

Don't

  • ✕Make the 2D area the only way to choose
  • ✕Let a marker vanish on a same-color background
  • ✕Rely on color alone for the selected preset
  • ✕Add alpha in the baseline unless a real use needs it

When to use

Use a date picker to choose a single calendar date such as an appointment or a due date, supporting both typing the date in the field and picking it in the calendar (NN/g date entry). The trigger is an Input field, so it inherits the field ladder, focus ring, sizes, and error and disabled states.

When not to use

Not for a date range, which is a separate range picker out of scope here; not for a time-only value, which is the TimePicker; not for a free number, which is an Input. At the sm size the field value text is 12px (D-170): reserve sm fields for dense, low-stakes desktop UI and do NOT use them for high-stakes data entry such as clinically important values; on mobile, sub-16px field text triggers iOS Safari auto-zoom (D-068), so re-floor field text at 16px there.

Behavior

The field trigger opens an anchored calendar popover that closes on Escape or outside-click and flips or shifts to stay on-screen, with no scrim and no focus trap (the overlay model, D-155). The selected day is a filled accent circle and today is an outline ring in the same accent, distinct by shape so the cue is never color alone (WCAG 1.4.1). Out-of-month days show muted in text.secondary and stay selectable, and a typed date in the field is accepted. Motion follows D-037 and drops to opacity-only under reduced motion.

Keyboard

In the field, type a date; in the grid, arrow keys move by day, PageUp and PageDown move by month, Home and End jump to the week ends, Enter selects, and Escape closes and returns focus to the field, following the WAI-ARIA APG date-grid pattern.

Content

Keep the label a short noun and show the expected format as a real example or in helper text, never only in the placeholder (NN/g, D-049). Use synthetic dates in any example and never a real date tied to a person.

Do

  • ✓Allow both typing and picking
  • ✓Mark today with a ring, distinct from the filled selected day
  • ✓Keep out-of-month days visible and selectable
  • ✓Return focus to the field on close

Don't

  • ✕Rely on color alone to show selected vs today
  • ✕Use text.disabled for out-of-month days (it fails AA in Dark)
  • ✕Trap focus in the calendar (it is a non-modal popover)
  • ✕Put the date format only in the placeholder

When to use

Use a divider when grouping needs an explicit line, after reaching for whitespace first; whitespace groups content more quietly and a rule is the heavier tool (NN/g visual hierarchy). A horizontal rule separates stacked content sections; a vertical rule separates inline items. A labeled divider names the section it opens, with the label bound to ui/caption (D-089) and color.text.secondary, sentence case.

When not to use

Not where spacing alone already groups content - prefer Space (NN/g). Not as decoration. Not to fake a heading: use a real heading recipe, never the label/sm overline (D-090). Bind the line to border.default, never border.subtle, which vanishes on the default Dark surface (1.00:1, D-109).

Behavior

A decorative rule exposes role=separator with aria-orientation, or is a native <hr>; a labeled rule is a plain container whose label is real content (WCAG 1.3.1). The line binds border.default at border.width.default (1px) so it stays visible in Light and Dark (D-109); margins bind the spacing ramp (spacing.6 unlabeled, spacing.4 labeled, spacing.2 for a vertical rule). Solid is the default; use dashed sparingly for a softer or provisional break (a CSS border-style flag, no token). No interaction state, no focus, no motion (D-107).

Content

The on-rule label is short and sentence case (D-089/D-091); it names a section, so it uses ui/caption, never the label/sm overline (D-090). Keep it to a couple of words. Synthetic example: a Schedule section rule labeled Today.

Do

  • ✓Prefer whitespace, then a divider when grouping needs a line
  • ✓Expose role=separator on a decorative rule
  • ✓Keep an on-rule label short and sentence case
  • ✓Use the vertical divider for inline item separation

Don't

  • ✕Use a divider where spacing alone would group (prefer Space)
  • ✕Bind the line to border.subtle - it vanishes on the default Dark surface (D-109)
  • ✕Label a divider with the label/sm overline recipe (D-090)
  • ✕Overuse dashed rules

When to use

Use a drawer for a side panel of detail, history, or a secondary task that should overlay the page without leaving it - the audited screens slide detail, history, and attestations in from the right.

When not to use

Not for a centered decision, which is a Modal; not for a small anchored panel, which is a Popover; not for a transient notice, which is a Notification.

Behavior

The drawer slides from the edge (default right) on a scrim that dims and blocks the page, traps focus and returns it to the trigger on close (APG dialog), and closes on Escape or a scrim-click. It slides per D-037 and drops to opacity-only under reduced motion. Prefer not to disable a control; where one must be, say why (D-097).

Keyboard

Focus enters the drawer on open; Tab and Shift+Tab cycle within it (the trap); Escape closes it; and focus returns to the trigger on close (APG dialog).

Content

Give the drawer a clear title and a close, and put actions in the footer. Use synthetic content in any example.

Do

  • ✓Slide from the edge on a scrim and trap focus
  • ✓Return focus to the trigger on close
  • ✓Use a clear title and footer actions
  • ✓Default to the right edge

Don't

  • ✕Use a Drawer for a centered decision - that is a Modal
  • ✕Use a Drawer for a small anchored panel - that is a Popover
  • ✕Let a scrim-click discard unsaved work
  • ✕Convey the open state by motion alone

When to use

Free-text values: names, notes, numbers, search terms. Use variant=search for search fields in content (branded muted fill). Use multiline for paragraph-length entry. Use size lg (44) in touch and mobile contexts.

When not to use

Not for choosing from a known set (use Select); not for binary toggles (Checkbox/Switch); not for the global top-bar search, which is chrome (topbar/input-* tokens), not this component. Filter pills and segmented capsule controls are out of scope (D-050 Q8). At the sm size the value text is 12px (D-170): reserve sm fields for dense, low-stakes desktop UI and do NOT use them for high-stakes data entry such as clinically important values; on mobile, sub-16px field text triggers iOS Safari auto-zoom (D-068), so re-floor field text at 16px there.

Behavior

Fill identifies the field at rest (WCAG 1.4.11 satisfied by fill + persistent label; the quiet border is not load-bearing). Border escalates on hover; the ring arrives on focus/active. Error border is 1px so it never competes with the 2px focus ring; focused-and-invalid shows both. Placeholder disappears on input and is never the sole label. Disabled suppresses focus and hover. Validation fires on blur or after a short pause (about 300 to 500ms), never on every keystroke, so a half-typed value is not flagged mid-entry; success is confirmed inline, and both error and success are announced through a live region (aria-live), so state is never carried by color alone (NN/g, D-037).

Keyboard

Standard text editing: Tab focuses (ring appears), Shift+Tab reverses, typing inserts at caret. Multiline accepts Enter as a newline; single-line Enter submits per form context. The trailing xmark icon, when used, is the clear affordance.

Content

Labels are short noun phrases, sentence case, no trailing colon. Placeholder is a genuine example only (for example name@c8health.com), never a format hint, instructions, or the label; format hints live in persistent helper text so they do not vanish on the first keystroke (NN/g). Helper text is one sentence, 12px; if it reads small, move critical content into the label - size stays. Error text says what is wrong and how to fix it. Advisory, not a binding token rule: a field capturing the signed-in user's own identity data should set an HTML autocomplete token so browsers can autofill and assistive tech can announce its purpose (WCAG 1.3.5, Level AA); a patient-data field must instead disable autofill so one person's saved data never lands in another patient's record. The component does not emit this markup today; it is an app-integration concern flagged here.

Do

  • ✓Keep a visible label on every field
  • ✓Use the field group for label/helper
  • ✓Use lg in touch contexts
  • ✓Show both ring and red border when focused-and-invalid
  • ✓Validate on blur or after a brief pause, not on every keystroke
  • ✓Confirm success inline, not only on submit
  • ✓Size a field to its expected input length where the length signals the format (Baymard)

Don't

  • ✕Use a heavy resting border (the fill identifies the field)
  • ✕Use placeholder as the label
  • ✕Render error borders at 2px
  • ✕Italicize placeholder (italic is the inert disabled value only)
  • ✕Put this component in the top bar
  • ✕Reach past semantic to a primitive
  • ✕Flag an error while the user is still typing the value
  • ✕Put a format hint in the placeholder - it disappears on input; use helper text

When to use

Use InputNumber for a bounded number the user nudges by a step (counts, quantities, durations), with min / max / step set and the unit and range named in helper text (NN/g steppers).

When not to use

Not for an unbounded or free-form number that is faster typed (plain Input); not for a numeric identifier that is never incremented (Input); not for a wide continuous range that is easier to explore with a Slider; not for choosing from a set (Select). At the sm size the value text is 12px (D-170): reserve sm for dense, low-stakes desktop UI and do NOT use a sm number field for high-stakes data entry such as clinically important values; on mobile, sub-16px field text triggers iOS Safari auto-zoom (D-068), so re-floor field text at 16px there.

Behavior

Type, press the full-height minus / plus, or use ArrowUp / ArrowDown; the value clamps to min / max and moves by step, with the relevant stepper disabled at each bound; an out-of-range or non-numeric value is surfaced like an Input error and announced, never by color alone (WCAG 1.4.1, D-050). The steppers are always visible so the affordance is discoverable and works on touch. Prefer not to disable the field; where it must be disabled, convey why (D-097).

Keyboard

ArrowUp / ArrowDown step the value; typing edits it directly; the control exposes the spinbutton role with aria-valuenow / valuemin / valuemax, and each stepper button has an accessible name (Increase / Decrease). PageUp / PageDown for a larger step and Home / End for min / max are optional enhancements per the WAI-ARIA APG spinbutton pattern.

Content

Name the unit and the allowed range in helper text; use a real example number as the placeholder, never the label; keep the label a short noun phrase (NN/g).

Do

  • ✓Set min, max, and step so stepping and clamping work
  • ✓Name the unit and range in helper text
  • ✓Keep the steppers always visible and full height for touch
  • ✓Give each stepper an accessible name

Don't

  • ✕Use InputNumber for a number that is never incremented - use Input
  • ✕Put the unit or range only in the placeholder
  • ✕Hide the steppers behind hover
  • ✕Convey an out-of-range error by color alone

When to use

Use a message for a brief, transient confirmation of a direct action, such as Copied or Saved.

When not to use

Not for anything that needs a title, description, or a close, which is a Notification; not for an inline status, which is an Alert.

Behavior

A compact pill at top-center that fades after a generous moment and is announced via a live region (role=status). Status is conveyed by icon and text, never color alone (WCAG 1.4.1).

Keyboard

A message is not interactive and takes no focus; it is announced via the live region.

Content

Keep it to one short line. Use synthetic content in any example.

Do

  • ✓Keep it to one line
  • ✓Announce it via a live region
  • ✓Pair the status with an icon and text
  • ✓Use generous timing

Don't

  • ✕Put a title, description, or actions in a Message - use a Notification
  • ✕Convey status by color alone
  • ✕Use it for an inline status
  • ✕Make it persist

When to use

Use a modal for a focused task or a decision of consequence that should interrupt the flow and be resolved or dismissed before continuing - a confirmation, a short form, or a destructive-action confirm that needs context (D-098). The modal blocks and dims the page until it is resolved, so reserve it for moments that genuinely warrant interruption (NN/g modal overuse).

When not to use

Not for non-blocking information - use a Notification or place the content inline; not for content that works better inline, which modal overuse buries (NN/g); not for a small anchored panel of secondary content (Popover) or a side panel of detail and history (Drawer); never stack or nest modals.

Behavior

The modal opens centered on a scrim that dims and blocks the page. Focus moves into the dialog, is trapped while it is open, and returns to the trigger on close, following the WAI-ARIA APG dialog pattern. Escape and a scrim-click both close it by default; but a modal that holds unsaved edits sets a blocking flag so a stray scrim-click cannot lose work - it offers Save or Discard instead, a clinical data-loss guard (D-154). The open and close animate as a fade plus a 0.98-to-1 scale and drop to opacity-only under reduced motion (D-037). Prefer not to disable an action; where one must be disabled, say why (D-097).

Keyboard

Focus enters the dialog on open; Tab and Shift+Tab cycle within it (the focus trap); Escape closes it; and focus returns to the triggering control on close (WAI-ARIA APG dialog).

Content

Give the modal a clear, specific title, a concise body, and verb-labelled actions with the primary action trailing; keep the whole thing short. Use synthetic content in any example and never real patient data.

Do

  • ✓Trap focus and return it to the trigger on close
  • ✓Use a clear title and verb-labelled actions
  • ✓Block dismissal when there are unsaved edits, offering Save or Discard
  • ✓Reserve modals for tasks that must interrupt

Don't

  • ✕Use a modal for non-blocking info - use a Notification
  • ✕Stack or nest modals
  • ✕Let a stray scrim-click discard unsaved work
  • ✕Convey the open state by motion alone

When to use

Use a notification for a transient, dismissible notice about a background or completed event - saved, sync finished, an error occurred - that should not block the page.

When not to use

Not for an inline status tied to a region, which is an Alert; not for a blocking decision, which is a Modal; not for a one-line transient confirm, which is a Message.

Behavior

It stacks at top-right and auto-dismisses on a generous timer but always offers a manual close, and it is announced via a live region (assertive for errors, polite otherwise). Status is conveyed by icon and text, never color alone (WCAG 1.4.1). Motion follows D-037.

Keyboard

Tab reaches the close button, which has an accessible name; the notice is announced via the live region.

Content

Give a short title and a one-line description. Use synthetic content in any example.

Do

  • ✓Keep a manual close
  • ✓Use role=alert only for errors
  • ✓Keep it short
  • ✓Pair the status with an icon and text

Don't

  • ✕Rely on auto-dismiss alone
  • ✕Convey status by color alone
  • ✕Use it for a blocking decision
  • ✕Stack endless notices

When to use

Use a popconfirm for a lightweight confirm of a single, low-to-moderate-risk action anchored to its trigger, such as deleting a row or discarding a draft.

When not to use

Not for a high-consequence or irreversible action, which needs a Modal confirm with more context (D-098); not for a non-decision message, which is a Tooltip or Notification.

Behavior

An anchored alertdialog that traps focus; Escape cancels, and Confirm and Cancel are real Buttons. A destructive confirm uses the destructive Button and prefers undo where the action is reversible (D-098). It scales from the anchor on open and drops to opacity-only under reduced motion (D-037).

Keyboard

Focus enters on open; Tab cycles within (the trap); Escape cancels; focus returns to the trigger on close (APG alertdialog).

Content

Keep the question short and label the confirm with the verb. Use synthetic content in any example.

Do

  • ✓Keep the question short
  • ✓Label the confirm with the verb
  • ✓Use the destructive style for destructive confirms
  • ✓Trap focus and let Escape cancel

Don't

  • ✕Use a Popconfirm for an irreversible high-cost action - use a Modal
  • ✕Rely on it for content
  • ✕Convey the decision by color alone
  • ✕Leave the confirm unlabeled

When to use

Use a popover for contextual content or secondary actions anchored to a trigger that do not need to block the page - settings, a secondary detail, a small form, or an info panel that should sit beside the thing it describes.

When not to use

Not for a blocking decision, which is a Modal; not for a plain text hint, which is a Tooltip; not for a lightweight inline confirm, which is a Popconfirm; not for primary navigation.

Behavior

The popover opens on a click or focus of its trigger with no page scrim and no focus trap, so focus can move into interactive content and the rest of the page stays usable. It opens on its preferred side and auto-flips or shifts to stay fully within the viewport on collision, so it never opens partly off-screen (D-155). Escape closes it and returns focus to the trigger, and a click or blur outside dismisses it. It scales from the anchor on open and drops to opacity-only under reduced motion (D-037).

Keyboard

Open it from the trigger, which carries aria-haspopup and aria-expanded; Tab moves through the content and then back out, since there is no trap; Escape closes it and returns focus to the trigger.

Content

Keep the content short, and if it holds actions, make them Buttons. Use synthetic content in any example.

Do

  • ✓Anchor to the trigger and flip or shift to stay on-screen
  • ✓Close on Escape and outside-click and return focus
  • ✓Use it for non-blocking contextual content

Don't

  • ✕Use a Popover for a blocking decision - that is a Modal
  • ✕Trap focus (it is non-modal)
  • ✕Let it open partly off-screen

When to use

Use progress to show a determinate task such as an upload or a batch action, or an indeterminate wait, always pairing the bar with a visible value so the state is readable without relying on the bar length or color.

When not to use

Not for a tiny inline indeterminate wait, which is a Spin; not for a content-shaped wait where the layout is known, which is a Skeleton.

Behavior

The fill is the accent throughout and is always paired with a visible numeric or text value; completion and failure are shown by an adjacent label or icon, never by recoloring the bar (WCAG 1.4.1). The fill animates its width, and an indeterminate animation drops to a static or reduced treatment under reduced motion (D-037). The component exposes role=progressbar with aria-valuenow.

Keyboard

Progress is not interactive and takes no focus; it is announced to assistive tech via role=progressbar and aria-valuenow.

Content

Always show a numeric or text value beside the bar and name what is progressing, for example Uploading report.pdf. Use synthetic content in any example.

Do

  • ✓Always show a numeric or text value
  • ✓Keep the fill the accent throughout
  • ✓Announce progress via aria-valuenow
  • ✓Show complete and failed by an adjacent label or icon

Don't

  • ✕Convey done or failed by bar color alone
  • ✕Animate an indeterminate bar under reduced motion
  • ✕Hide the value
  • ✕Recolor the bar to signal an error

When to use

Use a radio group for a small set of mutually exclusive options the user should see at once (about 7 or fewer, NN/g); single-select within a named group, one choice at a time. Above about seven options, use a Select instead.

When not to use

Not for non-exclusive choices, which are Checkboxes; not for a binary on/off, which is a Switch or a single Checkbox; not for a long enumerable list, which is a Select (NN/g).

Behavior

The selected state is carried by the filled dot, never by the fill color alone (D-055, WCAG 1.4.1), so it survives greyscale and color-blindness; hover, focus, and disabled are code-applied and focus is the shared 2px ring (D-040); the unselected circle border is deliberately darker because the boundary is the only cue that a control is there (WCAG 1.4.11, color.border.control). Selecting one option deselects the rest. Prefer not to disable a radio; where it must be disabled, convey why (D-097).

Keyboard

The group is one tab stop and arrow keys move and select among options with a roving tabindex; Space selects the focused option (WAI-ARIA APG radio-group pattern, not the checkbox model). Expose role=radiogroup and role=radio with aria-checked.

Content

Option labels are short, sentence case, and parallel in phrasing, one idea per option, with the label itself clickable (NN/g); the group label names the decision in the field-group, not in each option.

Do

  • ✓Use a radio group for one choice from a small mutually exclusive set
  • ✓Keep the set to about seven or fewer visible options
  • ✓Keep the dot as the selected cue, not color alone
  • ✓Make the whole row a click target that meets the touch minimum
  • ✓Use arrow keys to move and select within the group

Don't

  • ✕Use radios for non-exclusive choices - those are Checkboxes
  • ✕Use a radio group for a binary on/off - that is a Switch
  • ✕Use radios for a long list - that is a Select
  • ✕Rely on fill color alone to show the selection
  • ✕Disable a radio with no reason shown (D-097)

When to use

Use a result for a full-page outcome of a flow, such as a submission complete, a no-access page, or a not-found page, where the whole view is the feedback.

When not to use

Not for an inline status tied to a region, which is an Alert; not for a transient confirmation, which is a Message.

Behavior

The outcome is carried by the icon shape plus the title text plus the description, never by color alone (WCAG 1.4.1). The actions are real Buttons with the primary action trailing.

Keyboard

Tab reaches the Button actions; the Result region itself is not focusable.

Content

Give a short title, a one-line description, and one or two clear actions. Use synthetic content in any example.

Do

  • ✓Pair the outcome icon with a clear title
  • ✓Keep the description to one line
  • ✓Offer one or two clear Button actions
  • ✓Convey the outcome by icon shape and text, not color

Don't

  • ✕Use a Result for an inline status - that is an Alert
  • ✕Rely on color alone for the outcome
  • ✕Crowd the page with many actions
  • ✕Use a Result for a transient confirmation

When to use

Choosing from a known, enumerable set: sites, specialties, protocols, roles. Use searchable=on for long clinical lists (type-to-filter). Use multi-select where several values apply (e.g. multi-site filtering). Use lg (44) in touch contexts.

When not to use

Not for free text (Input); not for a small set of mutually exclusive choices the user should see at once - about 7 or fewer - which belong in a Radio group (NN/g); not for binary on/off (Switch/Checkbox); not for filter pills or segmented capsule controls (the mobile Sites filter bar - out of scope, D-050 Q8). At the sm size the trigger value text is 12px (D-170): reserve sm fields for dense, low-stakes desktop UI and do NOT use them for high-stakes data entry such as clinically important values; on mobile, sub-16px field text triggers iOS Safari auto-zoom (D-068), so re-floor field text at 16px there. (The menu option rows stay 14px regardless, C4/D-079.)

Behavior

The chevron is permanent - it is the affordance that says 'this opens a menu'; searchable does not remove it. Click or Enter/Space/ArrowDown opens the menu (motion/popover, 200ms decelerate in / 150ms accelerate out; chevron rotates 180). searchable=on makes the value slot a type-to-filter field using Input's typed-value behaviour; no-results renders one disabled row reading 'No matches'. Selecting closes single-select; multi-select keeps the menu open and persists checks. Multi values render as chips in the value slot; the trigger wraps vertically with the chevron pinned right. In multi-select, keep the chosen values visible as chips in the trigger so selections are never hidden behind a closed control; the shared check gutter carries the cue (D-055), but a single tick can read as single-select, so for a large option set or where selections must stay scannable while closed, prefer explicit checkbox rows or a checkbox list (NN/g). A disabled trigger follows the same rule as the button and field: prefer not to disable it, and where it must be disabled, convey why; disabled options inside the menu are skipped by keyboard and type-ahead (NN/g, APG).

Menu

min-width = trigger width; grows to the widest option, capped to viewport - NEVER truncate a clinical label by default. The only escape hatch for a genuinely unbounded string is per-instance truncate + tooltip.

Selection

The checkmark is the load-bearing cue (every tint is sub-3:1 against the menu) and MUST render in every selected row; the tint and bold label only reinforce. Dark's selected label is bold text/primary, not blue - the accent rides the check.

Keyboard

Tab focuses; Enter/Space/ArrowDown opens; Escape closes and returns focus; ArrowUp/Down move the active option; Home/End jump; Enter selects (and closes single-select); type-ahead matches (and filters when searchable); disabled options are skipped. ARIA role=combobox, aria-expanded, aria-controls; options role=option with aria-selected. Where the design allows, a native select is the most robust choice; the custom trigger must implement the full APG select-only combobox model described here (WAI-ARIA APG).

Content

Option labels short, scannable, never truncated by default. Placeholder names the choice ('Select a site'). 'No matches' is the canonical empty string.

Do

  • ✓Author Input first and reuse its field bindings verbatim
  • ✓Keep the check gutter in every row
  • ✓Let the menu grow rather than truncate
  • ✓Keep hover below selected in fill strength
  • ✓Keep multi-select choices visible as chips
  • ✓Use a Radio group instead when about 7 or fewer exclusive choices should be visible at once

Don't

  • ✕Invent select-specific field colors
  • ✕Rely on the tint as the selection cue
  • ✕Paint the menu shadow in Dark
  • ✕Remove the chevron in searchable mode
  • ✕Model trigger hover/active as variants
  • ✕Use this for filter pills or segmented controls
  • ✕Hide multi-select choices behind a closed trigger with no chips
  • ✕Disable the trigger with no reason shown

When to use

Use a skeleton for a content-shaped wait where the layout is known, such as a list or card loading, so the eventual content does not cause a layout shift.

When not to use

Not for a quick indeterminate wait, which is a Spin; not for a determinate task, which is a Progress bar.

Behavior

The placeholder blocks sit in the shape of the real content and a decorative shimmer sweeps across them; under reduced motion the shimmer is removed and the blocks are static (D-037). The region is aria-busy and the shimmer is aria-hidden, so the skeleton conveys only the loading state.

Keyboard

A skeleton is not interactive and takes no focus; the region is aria-busy.

Content

Match the skeleton to the real content shape so the layout does not shift when the content loads. Use synthetic shapes in any example.

Do

  • ✓Match the skeleton to the real content shape
  • ✓Go static under reduced motion
  • ✓Mark the region aria-busy
  • ✓Keep the shimmer decorative and aria-hidden

Don't

  • ✕Shimmer under reduced motion
  • ✕Use a skeleton for a long wait with no known shape
  • ✕Convey information in the skeleton beyond loading
  • ✕Use a skeleton for a determinate task

When to use

Reach for Space to gap inline or stacked elements on the spacing ramp instead of hand-written margins; consistent spacing groups related controls and aids scanning (NN/g; Gestalt proximity). small / middle / large cover the common rhythm and bind spacing.2 / spacing.4 / spacing.6 (D-108). Use a horizontal Space for inline rows of controls and a vertical Space for form and list stacks.

When not to use

Not for page-level column structure, which is Grid; not for a labeled or decorative separator between sections, which is Divider; not as a substitute for a component's own internal padding. A custom gap stays on the spacing ramp; if the ramp lacks the step you need, flag a mint rather than passing a literal (D-108).

Behavior

Pure layout: no interaction state, no focus, no motion (D-107). The gap, direction, alignment, and wrap are set by props, and the gap always resolves to a spacing-ramp step. Visual order matches DOM order so assistive tech reads items in the order they appear (WCAG 1.3.2).

Content

Space has no content of its own; it styles the spacing between the items it wraps, not text.

Do

  • ✓Bind every gap to a spacing-ramp step (D-108)
  • ✓Use Space for inline and stacked rhythm instead of ad-hoc margins
  • ✓Use a vertical Space for form and list stacks
  • ✓Flag a spacing mint when the ramp lacks a needed step

Don't

  • ✕Pass an arbitrary px gap off the spacing ramp (D-108)
  • ✕Use Space for page column layout - that is Grid
  • ✕Use Space as a section separator - that is Divider
  • ✕Reach past the spacing ramp to a literal value

When to use

Use a spinner for a short, indeterminate inline wait where there is no measurable progress and no known content shape.

When not to use

Not for a determinate task, which is a Progress bar; not for a content-shaped wait where the layout is known, which is a Skeleton.

Behavior

The ring rotates to signal the wait, but under reduced motion the animation stops and the ring is shown static at reduced emphasis, so the state is never conveyed by motion alone (D-037, WCAG 1.4.1). The region carries aria-busy and role=status with an accessible label so assistive tech is informed.

Keyboard

A spinner is not interactive and takes no focus; it is announced via role=status and aria-busy.

Content

Give the spinner an accessible label such as Loading and reserve it for short waits. Use synthetic content in any example.

Do

  • ✓Give it an accessible label
  • ✓Honor reduced motion with a static state
  • ✓Reserve it for short indeterminate waits
  • ✓Announce it via role=status and aria-busy

Don't

  • ✕Use a spinner for a determinate task
  • ✕Rely on motion alone to convey the state
  • ✕Use a spinner for a long wait with a known shape
  • ✕Spin under reduced motion

When to use

Use a switch for a binary system setting that takes effect the moment it is flipped, with no separate submit - notifications on or off, a feature toggle, a visibility preference (NN/g toggle guidance, which reserves the switch for immediate-effect settings). Default is the 44 x 24 size; small (28 x 16) suits dense settings rows, and the clickable row (the control plus its label) clears the WCAG 2.5.8 24px target at either size (D-132).

When not to use

Not for a choice that is submitted with a form rather than applied at once - that is a Checkbox (NN/g toggle-vs-checkbox); not for a set of mutually exclusive options - that is a Radio group; not for anything beyond a single on or off, such as choosing from an enumerable list - that is a Select.

Behavior

Flipping the switch applies the change immediately. The state is carried by the thumb position plus the track color together, never by color alone (D-055, WCAG 1.4.1), so it survives greyscale and color blindness; both the track-against-surface and the thumb-against-track boundaries clear the WCAG 1.4.11 3:1 non-text bar in Light and Dark because the on-track and on-thumb lighten in Dark (D-131). Hover and focus are code-applied and focus is the shared 2px ring around the track (D-040). The thumb slide animates at motion.hover and drops to instant under reduced motion, with state never conveyed by motion alone (D-037). Prefer not to disable a switch; where it must be disabled, convey why in adjacent text (D-097).

Keyboard

Tab moves focus to the switch and Space toggles it (Enter may also toggle on some platforms); the control exposes role=switch with aria-checked true or false, following the WAI-ARIA APG switch pattern, and the label is part of the click target.

Content

Label the switch by what it controls, phrased as the on-meaning in sentence case (Email notifications, not Do not email), one setting per switch (NN/g); the track itself carries no inner on/off text or icon (D-134), so the label beside it via the field-group is the only text (D-065).

Do

  • ✓Use a switch for a setting that applies immediately
  • ✓Put a clear on-meaning label beside it via the field-group
  • ✓Keep thumb position plus track color as the state cue
  • ✓Keep the row hit target at the 24px minimum at both sizes

Don't

  • ✕Use a switch for choices submitted with a form - that is Checkbox
  • ✕Use a switch for mutually exclusive options - that is Radio
  • ✕Rely on track color alone to show state
  • ✕Disable a switch with no reason shown (D-097)

When to use

Use a time picker to choose a time-of-day value such as an appointment time or a reminder, supporting both typing the time in the field and picking it from scrollable hour and minute columns. The trigger is an Input field, so it inherits the field ladder, focus ring, sizes, and error and disabled states.

When not to use

Not for a date, which is the DatePicker; not for a duration or a free number, which is an Input or InputNumber; a combined date-and-time picker composes the two and is out of scope here. At the sm size the field value text is 12px (D-170): reserve sm fields for dense, low-stakes desktop UI and do NOT use them for high-stakes data entry such as clinically important values; on mobile, sub-16px field text triggers iOS Safari auto-zoom (D-068), so re-floor field text at 16px there.

Behavior

The field trigger opens an anchored time popover that closes on Escape or outside-click and flips or shifts to stay on-screen, with no scrim and no focus trap (the overlay model, D-155). The selected time cell is a filled accent cell, so selection is the fill plus the value digits and never color alone (WCAG 1.4.1). A typed time in the field is accepted, and there is no today equivalent. Motion follows D-037 and drops to opacity-only under reduced motion.

Keyboard

In the field, type a time; in the columns, ArrowUp and ArrowDown move within a column, ArrowLeft and ArrowRight move between columns, Enter selects, and Escape closes and returns focus to the field (WAI-ARIA APG).

Content

Keep the label a short noun and show the expected time format as an example or in helper text, never only in the placeholder (NN/g, D-049). The 12-hour-with-AM/PM versus 24-hour choice is a config. Use synthetic times in any example.

Do

  • ✓Allow both typing and picking
  • ✓Show the selected time by an accent fill plus the digits
  • ✓Return focus to the field on close
  • ✓Show the time format as an example, not only in the placeholder

Don't

  • ✕Rely on color alone to show the selected time
  • ✕Trap focus in the columns (it is a non-modal popover)
  • ✕Use a time picker for a date - that is the DatePicker
  • ✕Put the time format only in the placeholder

When to use

Use a tooltip for a brief, supplementary hint on an icon or control, shown on hover and focus.

When not to use

Not for essential information, which belongs in the UI; not for interactive content, which is a Popover; not for a status message, which is an Alert.

Behavior

The tooltip appears on hover and focus and stays per WCAG 1.4.13 - it is hoverable (you can move onto it without it vanishing) and persistent (it stays until dismissed or focus and hover leave) - and it is dismissible with Escape. It is an inverse dark chip anchored to its trigger, and it is never the only way to get essential information.

Keyboard

It appears on focus of the trigger and is dismissed with Escape (WCAG 1.4.13); the tooltip itself is not focusable.

Content

Keep it to a few words with no actions. Use synthetic content in any example.

Do

  • ✓Keep it short
  • ✓Trigger on hover and focus
  • ✓Make it dismissible and hoverable
  • ✓Use the inverse chip so it reads as a transient hint

Don't

  • ✕Put essential or interactive content in a tooltip
  • ✕Trigger on click only
  • ✕Make it the only source of essential info
  • ✕Let it vanish when the pointer moves onto it

When to use

Use upload to attach one or more files, by dragging onto the zone or clicking to browse, and show each file with its status and a remove control.

When not to use

Not for a single inline value, which is an Input; not for a non-file action, which is a Button; not for content that is not an attachment.

Behavior

The zone accepts drag and click and is keyboard-operable via the browse Button; on drag-over it shows a focus border plus a tint plus a label change, never color alone (WCAG 1.4.1). Each file row shows its status by icon and text - uploading via a Progress bar with a visible value, done via a success check, error via an error icon and a short reason; the remove is a ghost control that reddens on hover (D-057). The resting drop-zone border is quiet and sub-3:1, so the zone is identified by its icon and prompt text (D-049), not the border.

Keyboard

Tab reaches the browse Button (activate it to open the file dialog) and each remove button, and the file list is navigable; drag is an enhancement, not the only path.

Content

The prompt names the action and the accepted files (Drag files here, or browse); error reasons are short and specific (Too large, max 10 MB); use synthetic filenames in any example.

Do

  • ✓Offer both drag and click
  • ✓Show each file's status by icon and text
  • ✓Use a Progress bar with a visible value while uploading
  • ✓Give every remove control an accessible name

Don't

  • ✕Make drag the only way to upload
  • ✕Convey status by color alone
  • ✕Rely on the quiet drop-zone border alone to identify the target
  • ✕Hide upload progress

Version history

The live staging head, pinned on top, then every released bundle newest first. The staging head is the go/no-go for the next cut; each released row is diffed against its predecessor bundle. Token diffs, deltas, parity and contrast are recomputed at build time on the cells affected by changed tokens; version, date, attestation and decisions-in-effect come from each bundle manifest, and the one-line summary and decision chips from the changelog. This panel only displays it - it computes nothing. Click a row to see what changed.
